changes: Updated public submission log feed to query incidents directly while protecting submitter privacy.
Added automated PII allowlist regression test verifying submitter identity (reported_by) is not exposed.
Overhauled mobile UI & UX across all devices, maintaining the dossier design aesthetic.
Fixed automatic page zoom on iOS Safari by enforcing a 16px (1rem) minimum font size on all input fields, selects, and textareas.
Expanded interactive touch targets across forms, buttons, checkboxes, and the navigation menu to a minimum 44–48px height.
Refined container spacing and margins for smaller viewports (320px–480px), eliminating text clipping and awkward word wrapping in headings.
Integrated dashboard chart and terminal styling into the global theme design system.
Added a personal submission history log to the dashboard page — see all your past smell reports at a glance.
Each history entry shows the date, smell type, location, and whether it was submitted to Gov.UK.
'Can't Tell' submissions are included but clearly marked as not submitted to Gov.UK.
The history log is mobile-optimised: stacked cards on small screens, a full table on desktop.
Your additional notes are shown alongside each report where provided.
Added a personal notes field to the report form. Anything you add will be sent directly to the Government in your individual submission — it will not be shared with other community members.
The notes input is now hidden if you have already logged the current event.
Updated the submission confirmation message to clarify that the Government email may take some time to arrive.
Fixed text inputs and textareas scrolling sideways on mobile — text now wraps correctly.
Fixed scrolled page content appearing above the sticky header on mobile.
Updated the changelog versioning rules to consolidate changes before pushing to main.
Standardised smell classification types across the backend mock database, testing suites, and local development scripts.
Updated automation scraper logic to map smell categories dynamically based on reported smell types.
Fixed smell scraper to correctly map 'Unknown' smell reports to 'You cannot describe it' on GOV.UK form.
Added a 2-hour anti-spam window to group active incidents and prevent conflicting reports.
Implemented automatic internal filtering for duplicate and unknown smell reports.
Updated smell reporting dropdown options to include 'Can't tell' option.
Updated smell types guidance text and mapping for odor reports.
Fixed an issue where content could be visible above the sticky header when scrolling.
Fixed issue where the navigation menu couldn't be fully viewed on short screens.
Fixed a bug causing the menu button to toggle inconsistently.
Prevented large text blocks from sticking to the top and obscuring the page.
Updated homepage subject text to better reflect the tool's purpose.
Standardized sticky headers across all pages to prevent content overlap.
Mobile navigation redesign with FAB menu.
Created the dedicated changelog page to view update history.
Dynamically display the current version in the header of all pages.
Added a Changelog link to the footer across the site.
Added a dedicated changelog page to track website updates.
Improved the design of the footer.
Initial launch of the Barking Stink incident reporting tool.
